Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint; Declaration Of Emily R. Pincin
Off Calendar Jul-19-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF LESLIE BROWN's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion for leave to file First Amended Complaint is granted. There is a liberal policy in favor of amendment. "If the motion to amend is timely made and the granting of the motion will not prejudice the opposing party, it is error to refuse permission to amend." (Morgan v. Sup.Ct. (1959) 172 Cal.2d 527, 530.) Here, the motion is timely as it was made after recent discovery disclosures and the trial date is November 2, 2024. The proposed amendment is not prejudicial. Defendant has had sufficient time to consider the UCL claim and further meet and confer efforts are not legally required nor reasonably warranted.

Plaintiff to file the First Amended Complaint by July 26, 2024.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Rochelle C. East; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RCE)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint; Declaration Of Emily R. Pincin

PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LESLIE BROWN'S Motion To Compel Defendant Community Housing Partnerships Further Responses To Plaintiffs Requests For Admission, Set One; Request For Sanctions In Amount Of $2,000.00; Declaration Of Emily R. Pincin

PLAINTIFF LESLIE BROWN'S (HEREAFTER EITHER "PLAINTIFF" OR "BROWN") MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT COMMUNITY HOUSING PARTNERSHIP (HEREAFTER EITHER "DEFENDANT" OR "COMMUNITY") TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, SET ONE, NOS. 4, 5, 20 - 23 AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS FOLLOWS:

(1) DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO RFA NOS 4, 5, 20 - 23 ARE OVERRULED.

(2) WITHIN TEN(10) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, DEFENDANT WILL DELIVER TO PLAINTIFF WITHOUT OBJECTION, VERIFIED CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO THE ABOVE RFA SETTING FORTH FOR EACH EITHER (1) ADMIT SO MUCH OF THE MATTER INVOLVED IN THE REQUEST AS IS TRUE; (2) DENY SO MUCH OF THE MATTER INVOLVED IN THE REQUEST AS IS UNTRUE, OR (3) LACK OF INFORMATION OR KNOWLEDGE AS A REASON FOR A FAILURE TO ADMIT ALL ORR PART OF A REQUEST FOR ADMISSION, AND THAT A REASONABLE INQUIRY CONCERNING THE MATTER IN THE PARTICULAR REQUEST HAS BEEN MADE, AND THAT THE INFORMATION KNOWN OR READILY OBTAINABLE IS INSUFFICIENT TO ENABLE THAT PARTY TO ADMIT THE MATTER.

(3) TO THE EXTENT THAT ANY RESPONSE TO RFA NOS. 4, 5, 20 - 23 IS OTHER THAN AN UNQUALIED ADMISSION, DEFENDANT WILL PROVIDE THE INFORMATION REQUIRED BY FORM INTERROGATORY 217.1 AS TO EACH SUCH RFA

(4) NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED AGAINST ANY PARTY TO THIS LITIGATION.
COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF SHOULD PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER CONSISTENT WITH THIS TENTATIVE RULING, SUBMIT A COPY TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER A COPY IN WORDFORM TO THE COURT (rennepar85@gmail.com) IN ADVANCE OF THE HEARING ON 2/21/24 REGARDLESS WHETHER DEFENDANT HAS APPROVED THE ORDER.

proposed order submitted.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Jennifer Roux CSR#11033, Steno.com, 707-477-9599 rouxmer@me.com, Court Reporter (302/JPT)
